intent: Write an explainer on 'why discussions of AI on Reddit often get heated' grounded in encyclopedic definitions. You MUST research BOTH sources: (1) Reddit — browse /f/technology AND /f/science for posts on large language models, AI safety, job displacement, or AI in education. Collect at least 4 posts with title, score, comment count, and the dominant sentiment. (2) Wikipedia — open the articles on 'Artificial intelligence' and 'Large language model' (or a closely related page like 'Generative artificial intelligence') and extract 3-4 key definitions and historical milestones. The report must: (a) state in 1-2 paragraphs what AI and LLMs actually are per Wikipedia, (b) cluster the Reddit discussion into at least 3 distinct themes (e.g. hype, fear, utility) with citations, (c) analyze which Reddit concerns are historically grounded versus where the community may be conflating terms, (d) close with a neutral explainer a layperson could use. Do not invent statistics.
author_notes: Reddit (technology + science) + Wikipedia (AI, LLM articles). Theme clustering + fact-checking community claims.
